claim: ESMFoldExtractor subclasses BatchedFeatureExtractor and predicts a 3-D structure + per-residue pLDDT for every Syn3A CDS using facebook/esmfold_v1. Output schema mirrors the AlphaFold extractor with an esmfold_ prefix (9 float columns: pLDDT mean/std, disorder fraction, helix/sheet/coil fractions, sequence length, radius of gyration, has_structure) so downstream consumers swap backends without changing column contracts. Session 15 addition that bypasses the UniProt lookup which blocked the AlphaFold-DB path: ESMFold consumes AA sequence directly, so no accession map is needed.

value.name: esmfold_v1
value.pdb_parsing_delegated_to: cell_sim/features/extractors/alphafold_extractor.py::_features_from_pdb (same PDB -> 9-feature helper used by AFDB path; ESMFold just renames the output keys to the esmfold_ prefix)

source_detail: Session 15 pivot. Module + 8 sandbox-safe tests verifiable by reading the code and running pytest. Upstream ESMFold model: Lin et al. 2023 Science 379:1123 (Evolutionary-scale prediction of atomic-level protein structure); weights at facebook/esmfold_v1 on HuggingFace Hub. Structures are generated on-device by the notebook, not fetched from an external database.

context.supersedes_for_syn3a: alphafold_extractor (blocked by the UniProt-indexing gap for taxid 2144189)

caveats: Cache parquet is NOT yet populated. Session 15 ships the extractor module + Colab notebook update; the actual populate run needs a GPU (user-side Colab).

ESMFold structure predictions are lower-confidence than AFDB's AlphaFold2-Multimer predictions for well-studied proteomes, but higher-confidence than nothing — and nothing is what the AFDB path delivers for a UniProt-missing synthetic organism like Syn3A.
Secondary-structure classification stays a torsion-bin proxy for DSSP (same helper as the AlphaFold extractor). Treat helix / sheet / coil fractions as ordinal and ~10-15 percent noisier than DSSP.

Fresh ESMFold inference at fp16 can vary run-to-run within ~1 Angstrom on radius of gyration and ~2 pLDDT points on the mean. The cache parquet's SHA-256 will differ between populate runs even on the same hardware; re-verification after re-populating requires refreshing the manifest.

Does NOT replace the AlphaFold extractor in the repo — both modules coexist. The default registry in cell_sim/layer6_essentiality/tier1_xgb_detector.py will swap in esmfold_v1 once the parquet is populated; callers who still want AFDB data can register the AlphaFold source independently.
No MCC measurement is claimed. A v13 / v14 measured fact will document any detector built on top of these features.
